mentions.note: 7.56% yield at unsustainable levels; market pricing probability of dividend cut; soup company no longer valued.

Down 22% YTD with 7.2% yield — skyhigh dividend signal that market doubts company can sustain payout. Packaged food industry needs consolidation to survive; no growth, tariff headwinds, no pricing power.
Only $7.7B market cap despite Rao's success; 6% yield safe but earnings expected down 2026; money managers avoid stocks with declining earnings despite good dividend.
7.4% yield not enough to attract buyers in stagflation scare; sector under pressure regardless of dividend appeal.
Food company considerably lower; inflation indicator; packaged food weakness.
70% yield too risky and illusory; Cramer never buys high-yield dividend plays; prefers McCormick in food space.
